payload.content: I'll create this data processing pipeline by delegating the generator and analyzer to sub-agents, then combining them into main.py.
payload.tool_calls: [ChatMessageToolCall(id='call_c01660af363c4f2883fce96c', function=ChatMessageToolCallFunction(name='delegate_task', arguments='{"expected_deliverable": "generator.py script that creates a CSV with 100 rows of mock sales data (Date, Product, Price, Quantity)", "instructions": "Create a Python script called generator.py that:\\n- Generates a CSV file named \'sales_data.csv\' with 100 rows of mock sales data\\n- Columns: Date, Product, Price, Quantity\\n- Use reasonable mock data (random dates in 2024, random products from a list like [\'Widget A\', \'Widget B\', \'Gadget X\', \'Gadget Y\'], random prices 10-100, random quantities 1-20)\\n- Include proper CSV headers\\n- Make it runnable as a standalone script", "task_name": "generator"}'), TYPE='function'), ChatMessageToolCall(id='call_aabe26d1668746c6ba01c3a0', function=ChatMessageToolCallFunction(name='delegate_task', arguments='{"expected_deliverable": "analyzer.py script that reads a CSV file and prints total revenue", "instructions": "Create a Python script called analyzer.py that:\\n- Reads a CSV file named \'sales_data.csv\' (with columns: Date, Product, Price, Quantity)\\n- Calculates total revenue (sum of Price * Quantity for all rows)\\n- Prints the total revenue in a readable format\\n- Make it runnable as a standalone script", "task_name": "analyzer"}'), TYPE='function')]
